        value=
      • value=(c: AnimationController; v: float32)

Animation primitives: Ticker, AnimationController, Tween, and a small set of built-in Curve functions. Models Flutter's package:flutter/animation.

Typical usage from inside a State:

# initState:
controller = newAnimationController(durationSec = 0.5)
controller.addListener(proc(v: float32) =
  setState(self, proc() = self.fade = v))
controller.forward(globalBinding, curve = curveEaseInOut)

# dispose:
controller.dispose()

Imports

../foundation/binding

Types

AnimationController = ref object
  lower*: float32
  upper*: float32
  duration*: float32
  status*: AnimationStatus
  ticker*: Ticker
  listeners*: seq[proc (v: float32)]
  statusListeners*: seq[proc (s: AnimationStatus)]
Drives a value field between lower and upper over duration seconds. Listeners are called on every value change; status listeners on every state transition. 
AnimationStatus = enum
  asDismissed, asForward, asReverse, asCompleted
Lifecycle states of an AnimationController. Matches Flutter:
  • asDismissed: at the lower bound, not running.
  • asForward: currently animating from lower toward upper.
  • asReverse: currently animating from upper toward lower.
  • asCompleted: at the upper bound, not running.
Curve = proc (t: float32): float32
An easing function. Receives t in [0, 1] and returns the eased progress (usually also in [0, 1], but curves like curveElasticIn may overshoot). 
Ticker = ref object
  callback*: TickerCallback
  active*: bool
  start*: float
A self-rescheduling frame callback. Used by AnimationController to advance value each frame. Stopped via Ticker.stop. 
TickerCallback = proc (elapsed: float32)
A frame-by-frame callback. elapsed is seconds since the ticker started. 
Tween[T] = object
  begin*, `end`*: T
A pair of values begin and end that can be interpolated by evaluating against an AnimationController. Works for any T that has a lerp(T, T, float32) -> T overload in scope; flit ships overloads for int, float/float32, Color, Offset, Size, EdgeInsets.

Procs

proc addListener(c: AnimationController; fn: proc (v: float32)) {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Registers fn to be called on every value change. Use this to trigger setState from a State that owns the controller. 
proc addStatusListener(c: AnimationController; fn: proc (s: AnimationStatus)) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Registers fn to be called on every AnimationStatus transition (asForward / asCompleted / asReverse / asDismissed). 
proc animateTo(c: AnimationController; b: Binding; target: float32;
               durationSec: float32 = -1.0; curve: Curve = curveLinear) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Animates from the current value to target over durationSec (or c.duration if negative). Status is set to asForward if target >= currentValue, otherwise asReverse. On arrival, status becomes asCompleted if target is at the upper bound, else asDismissed. 
proc curveBounceOut(t: float32): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Overshoots and bounces a couple of times before settling at 1. Mimics a rubber ball dropping. 
proc curveEaseIn(t: float32): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Slow start, fast end. f(t) = t^2. 
proc curveEaseInOut(t: float32): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Slow start and end, fast middle. Smooth S-curve. 
proc curveEaseOut(t: float32): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Fast start, slow end. f(t) = t * (2 - t). 
proc curveElasticIn(t: float32): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Rubbery start that pulls back before snapping forward. May return values outside [0, 1]. 
proc curveLinear(t: float32): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Constant-speed easing. Returns t unchanged. 
proc dispose(c: AnimationController) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Releases the ticker and clears all listeners. Call from State.dispose so the controller doesn't keep firing after its host widget unmounts. 
proc evaluate[T](tw: Tween[T]; c: AnimationController): T
Returns the interpolated value lerp(tw.begin, tw.end, c.value). 
proc forward(c: AnimationController; b: Binding; curve: Curve = curveLinear) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Drives the controller from lower to upper over duration seconds, applying curve to the progress fraction. Sets status to asForward immediately and asCompleted when it finishes. 
proc lerp(a, b: int; t: float32): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Linear interpolation between two ints. Truncates toward zero. 
proc lerp[T: SomeFloat](a, b: T; t: float32): T
Linear interpolation between two SomeFloat values. 
proc newAnimationController(durationSec: float32; lower = 0.0'f32;
                            upper = 1.0'f32): AnimationController {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Constructs an AnimationController.

Inputs:

  • durationSec: full sweep time in seconds. forward() and reverse() use this unless overridden by animateTo.
  • lower: initial / minimum value. Default 0.
  • upper: maximum value. Default 1.

Output: a fresh controller in asDismissed state with value = lower.

proc newTicker(cb: TickerCallback): Ticker {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Constructs a Ticker that will invoke cb each frame once started. The ticker is initially inactive; call start to begin. 
proc removeListener(c: AnimationController; fn: proc (v: float32)) {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Removes the first matching value listener. Matches by closure identity (the same proc(...) value must be passed back, same constraint as Flutter's removeListener). 
proc removeStatusListener(c: AnimationController; fn: proc (s: AnimationStatus)) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Removes the first matching status listener. 
proc repeat(c: AnimationController; b: Binding; curve: Curve = curveLinear;
            reverse: bool = false) {....raises: [Exception],
                                     tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}

Drives the controller from lower to upper repeatedly.

Inputs:

  • b: binding the ticker schedules against.
  • curve: easing applied within each lap.
  • reverse: if true, alternates direction each lap (ping-pong: lower->upper, then upper->lower, then lower->upper, ...). Default false (always restart at lower).

Effect: never sets status to asCompleted. Stop via controller.stop() or controller.dispose().

proc reverse(c: AnimationController; b: Binding; curve: Curve = curveLinear) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Drives the controller from its current value down to lower over duration seconds. Sets status to asReverse immediately and asDismissed when it reaches lower. 
proc start(t: Ticker; b: Binding) {....raises: [], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Begins ticking. Each frame, the ticker calls its callback with (now - t.start) and reschedules itself via b.scheduleFrame. Stops when t.active becomes false. 
proc stop(c: AnimationController) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Halts an in-progress animation. The current value is preserved (the animation is not "completed", just paused). 
proc stop(t: Ticker) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Stops the ticker on the next frame boundary. Subsequent scheduled callbacks become no-ops. 
proc tween[T](begin, ending: T): Tween[T]
Builds a Tween[T] from two endpoints. Use evaluate to read the interpolated value at the controller's current value. 
proc value(c: AnimationController): float32 {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Current value of the controller, always in [lower, upper]. 
proc value=(c: AnimationController; v: float32) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Sets the controller's value, clamped to [lower, upper]. Notifies all listeners if the value changed. Does NOT change status.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-09 04:30:19 UTC
